summary.intent: Merge main into the feat/deterministic-compaction branch and address outstanding PR review comments, specifically a Greptile P1 finding about threshold_percent being ignored in the auto-compact trigger.
summary.outcome: Main was merged (39 commits, one conflict resolved), the auto-compact trigger was wired to honor the configured threshold_percent instead of a hardcoded 95%, docs and schema were updated, 3 new tests added, and all changes pushed to PR #653 with the review thread resolved.

repo: The auto-compact trigger in crates/chat/src/lib.rs was hardcoded at 95% despite threshold_percent being a documented config field — the field was effectively a no-op before this fix.
load_prompt_persona_for_agent is the established pattern for resolving persona/config inside send() scope.
Test counts: chat crate had 227 tests pre-merge, main brought in 12 more (239), and the fix added 3 more (242); config crate stayed at 185.
The extract_summary_body function needed to iterate in reverse to pick the newest summary during iterative structured re-compaction (fixed in commit 13791ee2).
Greptile runs automated P1 reviews against new commits and can file fresh comments after each push.

code.finding: Hardcoded 95% auto-compact trigger — replaced with a call to a new helper that reads threshold_percent from config.
New helper extracted near estimate_text_tokens to encapsulate the threshold math and make it unit-testable.
threshold_percent docstring was updated to accurately reflect actual behavior after the field was wired up.
Template comment updated to match corrected threshold_percent semantics.
Docs updated to remove over-promise about LLM modes and reflect accurate threshold_percent behavior.

workflow: Greptile P1 threads are resolved via GraphQL mutation using thread ID (e.g., PRRT_kwDOREW6tc56TYrC).
Merge conflicts from parallel test additions are resolved by combining both test sets rather than choosing one side.
After merging main, re-fetch PR review threads — new Greptile comments may have been filed against earlier commits that are now relevant.

friction: One merge conflict in crates/chat/src/lib.rs where both branches added tests in the same location — required manual resolution.
Greptile filed a P1 after the initial push, requiring an additional fix cycle before the PR could be considered clean.
open_items: Greptile may re-run against the new commit (fb9167a0 or the threshold fix commit) and file additional comments — flagged explicitly to monitor.

The trace message and broadcast payload were updated to include the resolved threshold value for observability, but downstream consumers of that payload may need to be updated if they expect a fixed 95% value.
